channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: World Cup 2026: Germany book knockout spot with comeback win over Ivory Coast
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Germany book knockout spot with comeback win over Ivory Coast
item.description: Deniz Undav scored twice after coming off the bench as Germany sealed a thrilling 2-1 comeback victory over Ivory Coast in Toronto on Saturday to reach the World Cup knockout phase for the first time since lifting the trophy in 2014.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Farewell, Scotland: Boston won’t forget the Tartan Army
item.description: They arrived with kilts, songs and endless optimism. They leave behind unforgettable memories. For one week, Boston belonged to the Tartan Army. As Scotland's supporters head to Miami, their next World Cup destination, they say goodbye to a city that embraced them from day one.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran says closed Strait of Hormuz as US deal hits obstacle ahead of Swiss talks
item.description: Iran said Saturday it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israel’s military campaign in Lebanon, while warning that upcoming talks with the United States in Switzerland are unlikely to advance unless the fighting stops. President Donald Trump countered by threatening U.S.-imposed tolls on shipping through the strategic waterway if a final agreement is not reached within 60 days.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Germany into last 32 after Undav double seals comeback win over Ivory Coast
item.description: Substitute Deniz Undav scored two goals, including a dramatic injury-time winner, as Germany secured a 2-1 comeback victory over Ivory Coast on Saturday to book their place in the knockout rounds of the World Cup for the first time since 2014. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: World Cup 2026: Brobbey, Gakpo score braces as rampant Netherlands crush Sweden
item.description: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two goals each as the Netherlands thumped Sweden 5-1 in Houston on Saturday to move top of Group F and put one foot in the World Cup knockouts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: A deal in 'dire straits'? Israel's former Prime Minister Ehud Olmert talks to France 24
item.description: Just days after President Trump hailed a 60-day peace agreement with Iran, elements of the deal appear to be unravelling. Tehran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, despite a provision in the agreement guaranteeing the safe passage of commercial vessels
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: France swelters under record-breaking temperatures
item.description: It's hot here in France. The country's weather service expects the current heatwave to be “widespread, prolonged and intense”, with temperatures in some areas potentially topping 40 degrees Celsius. Paris will still hold its Fete de la Musique celebrations, but local officials in other regions have cancelled the event.
item.dc:creator: Catherine VIETTE

item.title: Zelensky returns Polish award as WWII row strains alliance
item.description: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire after continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon
item.description: Iran has closed the strait of Hormuz – citing alleged ⁠violations of a ceasefire agreement ​by the US ‌and Israel. Tehran said the closure was the "first step" in response ​to what it described ​as breaches ​of commitments regarding the continuing clashes in Lebanon - and warned ​that further measures would be taken if "aggression" continued. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani, and FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump says Italy's Meloni asked 'over and over' for a photo with him as diplomatic spat escalates
item.description: US President Donald Trump turned up the heat on a diplomatic row with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Saturday, accusing her of repeatedly seeking a photograph with him at the G7 summit and blaming the clash between the two heads of state on tensions over Iran and NATO. Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Haiti and Türkiye eliminated, US through to knockouts
item.description: Team USA made it through to the knockout rounds thanks to their 2-0 win against Australia. Morocco quickly got a first goal past Scotland, as did Paraguay against Türkiye. Paraguay's Matias Glaraza scored 64 seconds in, a new 2026 World Cup record. Brazil finally got their first win of the tournament and powered three past Haiti in the first half. Tunisia is set to face off against Japan, in what is the 1000s World Cup game ever.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: 🔴 Live: Hormuz Strait closed over Israel attacks on Lebanon, Iran says
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon despite ceasefire, at least 16 killed
item.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed at least 16 people on Saturday, Lebanese Civil Defencesaid, hours after a ceasefire with Hezbollah suposedly took effect. ​Israel says it ‌was responding to projectiles fired by the Iran-backed group. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondant in Jerusalem, Noga Tarnopolsky.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: NGOs in Lebanon denounce Israeli attacks on medics amid ceasefire
item.description: On March 2nd, Hezbollah dragged Lebanon into the war when it launched rockets at Israel in what it said was revenge for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader. Since then, Israel's campaign has killed more than 3,900 people and displaced 20% of the population. The humanitarian situation on the ground is dire. Details from the ground with Dr Mina Naguib, an emergency physician with Doctors Without Borders in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Europe swelters in gruelling heatwave, authorities ramp up warnings
item.description: A severe heatwave is sweeping across much of Europe, prompting emergency meetings in France, nationwide warnings in Germany and strains on tourists and residents ​in Italy. Temperatures climbed to record levels, and are set to hit 40°C (105.0 °F). Details by Shirli Sitbon.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Poland strips Zelensky of top state honour over WWII dispute
item.description: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: African fashion takes centre stage at France's Galerie Lafayette
item.description: African fashion is no longer merely a source of inspiration for major international fashion houses. Today, it is establishing its own codes, its own brands and its own ambitions. To mark the ‘Africa Now’ event held at Paris’ Galeries Lafayette, FRANCE 24 welcoms a key figures in this movement: Adama N'Diaye, the Senegalese fashion designer behind Adama Paris.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Team USA through to the round of 32 as Morocco and Brazil deliver
item.description: After Mexico, the United States also punched their ticket to the World Cup Round of 32 on Saturday, thanks to a 2-0 victory over Australia. Morocco and Brazil, who faced off in their opening match, both secured wins as well, edging past Scotland (1-0) and cruising by Haiti (3-0), respectively.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Drone use expands in Sudan – and kills at least 1,000 civilians in 2026
item.description: According to the United Nations, more than 1,000 civilians have been killed in drone strikes in Sudan since the beginning of the year. There has been a sharp increase in the use of drone warfare – a tactic marking a turning point in the three-year-war, which has created what the UN deems the "world's worst humanitarian crisis." Details by FRANCE 24 regional correspondent Bastien Renouil.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Why are so many Brits in Paris for Fête de la Musique?
item.description:
Block parties all night, live music till early in the morning, and... British tourists?
In 2025, Paris's annual Fête de la Musique, the 'Festival of Music' marking the advent of summer, saw a wave of young Brits crossing the Channel to take part in the revelry.
What's that trend all about? FRANCE 24's Tanishk Saha explains.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Canadian beer prices test German fans' thirst at FIFA World Cup
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is in full swing, including in Canada. Toronto is hosting Saturday's match between Germany and the Ivory Coast. The German supporters are there and they’re angry – according to them, the price of beer is far too high. Story by Daniel Quinlan and Antoine Fenaux.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Could Israel lose Donald Trump’s support by continuing to drop bombs in Lebanon?
item.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least seven people, including two children, hours after reports emerged of a ceasefire agreement. The persistent fighting threatened an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to end the war in the Middle East. Could Israel' Netanyahu lose Trump's support if strikes continue? Analysis by Yossi Mekelberg, senior consulting fellow at MENAP, Chatam House, London.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hezbollah-Israel flare-up in Lebanon despite ceasefire announced by the USA
item.description: US officials have announced that a ceasefire is in effect between Israel and Hezbollah. President Donald Trump claimed he had asked Israel for the ceasefire. However, it seems to be more on paper than reality: attacks and retaliation from both sides continued to flare following its theoretical coming into force on Friday afternoon local time. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondents in Jerusalem and Beirut, Noga Tarnopolski and Renée Davis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
